name: Asymmetric Hero
why_it_works: The oversized headline dominates the left column, creating immediate hierarchy through scale alone — no heavy weight needed. The 7/5 asymmetric split gives the text more breathing room than a 50/50 split while the image's slight upward offset (-2rem) breaks the grid baseline, creating visual tension that keeps the eye moving. Negative space around the body copy forces the reader to slow down before reaching the CTA.
when_to_use: Landing pages for consumer products, creative agency sites, campaign launches, or any hero where the product has strong visual appeal and the copy is confident enough to carry the layout.
watch_out: This pattern lives or dies on the headline. Weak copy at small type size collapses the hierarchy. Also fails with low-contrast or detailed images where the image fights the text rather than supporting it.
